findings.summary: Small clinical trials of bitter gourd preparations in type 2 diabetes report modest additional reductions in fasting glucose when taken alongside standard oral hypoglycemic agents, raising the possibility of an additive glucose-lowering effect.

No direct pharmacokinetic interaction between metformin and Momordica charantia constituents has been demonstrated; metformin is not metabolized by major cytochrome P450 enzymes and is not a known substrate for charantin or related compounds.
findings.citation: Internal pharmacology synthesis based on standard metformin ADME data

findings.summary: Case reports describe symptomatic hypoglycemia in patients combining concentrated bitter gourd juice with sulfonylureas or metformin, particularly at high or unmeasured juice doses.
